key_risks: A hot or very weak June payrolls print on July 2 could sharply rotate sector leadership against defensives/financials.
Holiday-shortened, thin-liquidity week amplifies single-day gap risk around the jobs data and FOMC minutes.
Recent sector strength (healthcare, financials, industrials) could mean-revert given elevated positioning and limited independent catalysts in-window.

portfolio.rationale: Strongest broad-sector momentum with defensive character; +8.4% 30d, +4% 7d, low beta (0.37), near 52w high with limited drawdown, resilient to a soft payrolls print on July 2.
Steady uptrend (+6.9% 30d, 61.9% up-day share) with Q2 bank earnings approaching mid-July; low drawdown and moderate beta support continuation into the window.
Broad cyclical strength (+6.6% 30d, +1.75% 7d) supported by ISM manufacturing still expansionary at 53.3 and firm new orders; consistent up-day share.
Defensive ballast; +5.7% 30d, +7.1% vs SPY, very low beta (0.07) and smallest drawdown cushion the portfolio against a weak jobs report and holiday-thinned liquidity.
Core benchmark exposure to limit tracking error against the SPY scoring benchmark while the sleaders carry relative alpha.

portfolio_rationale: Tilt toward defensive-but-trending sectors (healthcare, financials, industrials) with a low-vol cushion, keeping benchmark exposure to control downside from a soft July 2 payrolls print and a holiday-shortened, thin-liquidity week.
rationale_summary: Overweight healthcare/financials/industrials for continued relative strength backed by macro data, buffered by low-vol and core SPX to limit tracking error.
